content.text: Odilitime shared a full conversation interview with Shaw, Founder of elizaOS, covering key topics including why models decide agent usability, agent benchmarks shifting into games, how simulation data builds trading competence, tokens turning ideas into dev budgets fast, and why reinforcement learning made models improve rapidly. The interview was well-received across multiple channels with positive reactions from the community.

Developer activity in the coders channel showed strong community engagement. A contributor submitted their first pull request to the elizaOS repository, fixing documentation in the readme, which was positively received. Stan shared progress on building a universal autoscaling solution for agents on the cloud that supports WhatsApp, Telegram, SMS, and all clients out of the box, with infrastructure that automatically scales agents based on demand.

Community members discussed Aivilization, an open-world simulation game for digital lives featuring AI agents that can live, work, socialize, fight, and post their own thoughts publicly. The concept generated interest, with suggestions that it could capture a large audience. It was noted that Cayden was working on elizatown, a similar concept.

Odilitime addressed late token migration requests from users who missed the 90-day deadline for migrating from ai16z to elizaOS tokens. He indicated plans to compile a list of late migration requests, noting complications because some users had already sold their ai16z tokens. Discussion ensued about how to handle these cases fairly, with suggestions that governance should be based on tokens physically held at the time of the snapshot.

Odilitime shared content highlighting ElizaOS as a secure agent framework that has been battle-tested over 3 years and is suitable for financial applications. Additionally, an e/acc versus d/acc discussion was shared featuring notable figures in the crypto and AI space.
